title: glochidion superbum
intro: Glochidion superbum, also known as the Yellow Corkwood or Golden Corkwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to tropical Asia, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Glochidion superbum can be made from its bark, leaves, or roots, and are often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or topical creams and ointments.
